title: botrypus anthemoides
intro: Botrypus anthemoides, also known as the resurrection fern, is a type of plant that grows in the southeastern United States, particularly in the freshwater swamps of North Carolina and South Carolina. It is known for its unique ability to survive underwater for long periods of time, only to revive when exposed to air. The medicinal properties of Botrypus anthemoides include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been used to treat various health issues, such as respiratory problems and skin infections. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and salves, which have been used to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and support immune function.
